questions.question: What is the deepest point in the ocean?
questions.explanation: The Challenger Deep in the Mariana Trench is ~11,034 m deep.

questions.question: Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean covers about 165.25 million km², more than all land combined.

questions.question: Which continent has the most countries?
questions.explanation: Africa has 54 recognized countries, more than any other continent.

questions.question: What is the largest desert in the world?
questions.explanation: Antarctica is technically the largest desert at 14.2 million km².

questions.question: Which ocean is the warmest?
questions.explanation: The Indian Ocean is the warmest ocean in the world.

questions.question: What percentage of Earth's surface is covered by water?
questions.explanation: About 71% of Earth's surface is covered by water.

questions.question: Which is the smallest continent by area?
questions.explanation: Australia is the smallest continent at about 7.7 million km².

questions.question: The Great Barrier Reef is located off the coast of which continent?
questions.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef is off the northeast coast of Australia.

questions.question: Which strait connects the Atlantic Ocean to the Mediterranean Sea?
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar connects the Atlantic to the Mediterranean.

questions.question: What is the longest mountain range on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Andes is the longest continental mountain range at about 7,000 km.

questions.question: Which continent is entirely in the Southern Hemisphere?
questions.explanation: Antarctica is the only continent entirely in the Southern Hemisphere.

questions.question: What is the saltiest body of water on Earth?
questions.explanation: Don Juan Pond in Antarctica is the saltiest at over 40% salinity.

questions.question: Which ocean surrounds the North Pole?
questions.explanation: The Arctic Ocean surrounds the North Pole.

questions.question: What is the largest island in the world?
questions.explanation: Greenland is the largest island at about 2.17 million km².

questions.question: The Ring of Fire is located primarily around which ocean?
questions.explanation: The Ring of Fire circles the Pacific Ocean with 75% of the world's volcanoes.
